content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 1, 1991


Robert Johnson
JOHNSON & JOHNSON
444 West Ocean Blvd., Suite 801
Long Beach, CA 90802

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-91-192

Dear Mr. Johnson:

We have received your letter dated March 25, 1991, seeking
advice on behalf of an unnamed official of an unidentified public
agency under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹

You were informed by telephone on April 3, 1991 that we were
unable to provide you with the assistance you requested for three
reasons. First, you failed to identify the official and the
official's agency in your letter. Second, you did not indicate
whether you were acting as the official's authorized
representative. And third, it was not clear from your letter
whether the inquiry addressed an issue of past conduct. You were
informed that the Commission provides advice that is prospective
in application and does not address issues concerning prior
conduct. (Regulation 18329, copy enclosed.)

You were also informed that because it was not clear from
your letter whether a pending governmental decision was involved,
whatever advice that could ultimately be provided would be
informal only.²

At the conclusion of the conversation it was apparent that
your reluctance to disclose the official's name and agency
affiliation was premised on the fact that the conduct about which

________________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear in 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Di-
vision 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Sec-
tion 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

File No. I-91-192
Page 2

you were inquiring had already taken place. Because of your
concern that the advice we might provide would conclude that the
conduct about which you inquired was not permitted under the Act,
you wished to refrain from disclosing the name of the official.

The concerns expressed in your letter referenced Section
87103.5, "Income from Retail Sales." At my suggestion, you were
sent by facsimile transmission a copy of Regulation 1870.5, the
Commission-adopted interpretative regulation for that section.
While you indicated that you would contact your client to
determine whether he wished to be disclosed in the advice request,
I will reiterate that to the extent your question with respect to
the Act concerns conduct which has already occurred, we will not
be able to provide you or the official with any assistance.

I hope this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. Please contact me if you have any further questions
regarding this matter at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:ken
Enclosures

sections.question_synthetic: An attorney sought informal advice on behalf of an unnamed official regarding conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, specifically regarding income from retail sales.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide informal advice because the requestor failed to identify the official and agency, did not confirm authorized representation, and the inquiry appeared to address past conduct rather than prospective matters; the FPPC clarified that it only provides prospective advice and cannot address prior conduct.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination/correspondence letter without formal Question and Conclusion sections. The document explains why the FPPC cannot assist rather than providing substantive legal advice. Some text is partially obscured or corrupted (indicated by ■ symbols), but the core meaning is clear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: An attorney sought informal advice on behalf of an unnamed official regarding conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, specifically regarding income from retail sales.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide informal advice because the requestor failed to identify the official and agency, did not confirm authorized representation, and the inquiry appeared to address past conduct rather than prospective matters; the FPPC clarified that it only provides prospective advice and cannot address prior conduct.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide informal advice to an attorney on behalf of an unnamed public official due to lack of identification, unclear authorization, and the apparent retrospective nature of the inquiry concerning conduct that has already occurred.
